Article.ArticleTitle: Mobilization with movement enhances early rehabilitation outcomes in knee osteoarthritis: a six-week randomized controlled trial.

AffiliationInfo.Affiliation: Department of Physical Therapy and Health Rehabilitation, College of Applied Medical Sciences, Majmaah University, Al-Majmaah, 11952, Saudi Arabia.

Rehabilitation Research Chair, Department of Rehabilitation Health Sciences, College of Applied Medical Sciences, King Saud University, P.O. Box. 10219, Riyadh, 11433, Saudi Arabia. physioamir@gmail.com.
Medical Rehabilitation Sciences Department, Faculty of Applied Medical Sciences, Umm Al-Qura University, Mecca, Saudi Arabia.
Department of Physical Therapy, College of Medical Rehabilitation Science, Taibah University, Madina, Saudi Arabia.
Department of Physiotherapy, School of Allied Health Sciences, Manav Rachna International Institute and Studies (MRIIRS), Faridabad, India.
Department of Basic Sciences, College of Medicine, Princess Nourah Bint Abdulrahman University, P.O. Box. 84428, Riyadh, 11671, Saudi Arabia.
Department of Rehabilitation Sciences, The Hong Kong Polytechnic University, Hong Kong, China.
Rehabilitation Research Chair, Department of Rehabilitation Health Sciences, College of Applied Medical Sciences, King Saud University, P.O. Box. 10219, Riyadh, 11433, Saudi Arabia.

Grant.Agency: Ongoing Research Funding program - Research Chairs (ORF-RC-2025-1000), King Saud University, Riyadh, Saudi Arabia

MedlineCitation.CoiStatement: Declarations. Ethics approval and consent to participate: The study was approved by the Ethics Sub-Committee, King Saud University, Riyadh, Saudi Arabia (approval ID: RRC-2021-17). The trial was prospectively registered with the ClinicalTrials.gov Protocol Registration and Results System (PRS) under the identifier: NCT05577403, dated: 12/07/2021 (initial release), and the record was subsequently updated on 14/09/2025 (https://clinicaltrials.gov/study/NCT05577403). The study adhered to the ethical standards of the World Medical Association Declaration of Helsinki (2010). All participants provided written informed consent prior to study enrolment. Consent for publication: Not applicable. Competing interests: The authors declare no competing interests.
